name: Reopen The PTX One-K 128x128 Hot-Band Control Branch

summary.name: Reopen The PTX One-K 128x128 Hot-Band Control Branch
summary.hypothesis: Round 1/20 already spent the first budget unit on a bounded retune of the active one-K 128x128 copy cadence, and node_a measured that branch slightly slower at 25.381776 ms versus the 25.325055 ms accepted base. The dominant hot-band kernel still owns about 32.918752 ms of device time with 196 registers per thread, only 16.65% active warps, 47.93% tensor-pipe activity, 7.60% long-scoreboard stall, and 5.75% barrier stall. The PTX one-K control branch keeps the same grouped-row 128x128 grid and hot-band split, but swaps the inner accumulate/export control path. That makes it the cleanest orthogonal next test because a prior PTX measurement already landed at 25.379312 ms, close enough that a small control-path win could be real on this surface.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Hot-band inner-loop control and export overhead in the standard one-K 128x128 path, rather than the peeled 64x384 residual rows or the 64x96 tail.
summary.risk: Moderate to high. The PTX WMMA path is correctness-sensitive, but it is already present in-tree and the implementation surface should stay small because node_c only needs to route the default hot-band launch onto the existing branch.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the current 25.381776 ms run and the prior PTX 25.379312 ms run
ranking_notes: The immediately preceding copy-cadence retune already regressed, so another in-family retune should not stay ranked first.
This branch is orthogonal while still preserving the current 128x128 geometry and split.
The prior PTX measurement was already near the current control run, which makes a quick A/B round plausible.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the PTX one-K branch does not beat the current 25.381776 ms run cleanly or if it regresses correctness/build despite staying on the same hot-band geometry.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
